walt_content: Discuss free time routines and active hobbies fluently. Shift away from Band 5.0 hesitation by using adverbs of frequency to describe habits naturally.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Provide sentence frames on the board if weaker students freeze during the speaking circuit: "My favorite pastime is ____ because..." and "I usually participate in ____." Allow them to rely on their Chinese translation table.
stretch_content: Demand exact execution of the complex sentence without reading. Direct fast-finishers to the Part 3 Differentiation Stretch question (Section 10) to force spontaneous, unscripted reasoning.
grammar_content:
• Adverbs: Place them before the main verb but after the 'be' verb (e.g., I usually play... / I am always happy...).
副词：放在实义动词之前，但在be动词之后 (例如: I usually play... / I am always happy...)

• Gerunds: Verbs expressing preference (enjoy, prefer, love) are followed by a V-ing gerund (e.g., I enjoy playing badminton...).
动名词：表达喜好的动词 (enjoy, prefer, love) 后面接 V-ing 形式 (例如: I enjoy playing badminton...)
pronunciation_content:
To sound like a native speaker, do not pause between words if one ends in a consonant sound and the next begins with a vowel sound. Slide them together!
为了听起来像母语者，如果一个单词以辅音结尾，下一个单词以元音开头，请不要停顿。把它们连起来读！

• Say: have‿a blast (Sounds like: ha - va - blast)

• Say: couch‿potato (Smoothly connect the ch into the p)
idioms: [{"phrase": "Couch potato", "meaning": "\u7535\u89c6\u8ff7 / \u61d2\u9aa8\u5934", "explanation": "\u6307\u559c\u6b22\u6574\u5929\u5750\u5728\u6c99\u53d1\u4e0a\u770b\u7535\u89c6\u3001\u4e0d\u7231\u8fd0\u52a8\u7684\u4eba\u3002", "example": "On weekends, I become a couch potato and watch TV shows all day.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Have a blast", "meaning": "\u5c3d\u60c5\u6b22\u4e50 / \u73a9\u5f97\u75db\u5feb", "explanation": "\u73a9\u5f97\u975e\u5e38\u5f00\u5fc3\uff0c\u5ea6\u8fc7\u4e86\u4e00\u6bb5\u6781\u597d\u7684\u65f6\u5149\u3002", "example": "We went to the music festival last night and had a blast.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What activities did you usually do to relax after a long day at middle school?", "Do you prefer spending your free time alone or hanging out with friends?", "Have your hobbies changed since you started Grade 10?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, finding free time is hard for Grade 10 students. Nowadays, our schedules are very busy with heavy homework loads. However, it is still very important to unwind. Usually, many of my classmates act like a couch potato on weekends. They just watch videos online and sleep all day. Personally, I prefer active recreation. For example, I am a huge basketball enthusiast, so I always participate in school matches. When I play sports with my friends, we always have a blast. Overall, staying active is a truly fascinating pastime.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a leisure activity you enjoy doing regularly. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What it is and what equipment is needed.", "Who you do it with.", "Explain why this is your ideal way to relax."]
model_answer: Well, my favorite pastime is playing badminton. Normally, I play it every Saturday afternoon at the local sports center. Actually, I rarely go alone, so I always participate with my best friends. To be honest, I used to be a couch potato before high school, but now, I am a huge sports enthusiast. Because high school is very stressful, I always get some exercise to unwind. In fact, running around the court is a fascinating form of recreation. To sum up, we highly enjoy this activity, and we always have a blast.

walt_content: Discuss memorable TV shows and movies using comparatives and superlatives. Evaluate content effectively using target vocabulary.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Provide sentence frames: "My favorite show is ____ because it is more..." and "I think documentaries are the most..." Allow them to rely on their Chinese translation table.
stretch_content: Demand exact execution of the complex sentence without reading. Direct fast-finishers to the Part 3 Differentiation Stretch question (Section 10) to force spontaneous comparing and evaluating.
grammar_content:
• Comparatives: Compare two things (e.g., News is more informative than cartoons. / This show is funnier than that one.).
比较级：比较两件事物 (例如: News is more informative than cartoons. / This show is funnier than that one.)

• Superlatives: Compare three or more things (e.g., It is the most entertaining show on TV. / He is the best actor.).
最高级：比较三件或更多事物 (例如: It is the most entertaining show on TV. / He is the best actor.)
pronunciation_content:
English words have one stressed syllable that is longer, louder, and higher in pitch. If you stress the wrong syllable, it can be hard to understand.
英语单词有一个重读音节，发音更长、更响亮、音调更高。如果你重读错误的音节，可能会很难理解。

• Say: In-FOR-ma-tive (Stress the 2nd syllable)

• Say: En-ter-TAIN-ing (Stress the 3rd syllable)

• Say: Ad-DIC-tive (Stress the 2nd syllable)
vocab_rows: [{"word": "Broadcast", "type": "V/N", "meaning": "广播 / 播出"}, {"word": "Commercial", "type": "N/Adj", "meaning": "商业广告 / 商业的"}, {"word": "Informative", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "提供有用信息的"}, {"word": "Entertaining", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "娱乐的 / 有趣的"}, {"word": "Subscribe", "type": "V", "meaning": "订阅 / 关注"}, {"word": "Addictive", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "使人上瘾的"}, {"word": "Unwind", "type": "V", "meaning": "放松 / 解压"}, {"word": "Fascinating", "type": "Adj", "meaning": "迷人的 / 极好的"}]
idioms: [{"phrase": "Glued to the screen", "meaning": "目不转睛 / 紧盯屏幕", "explanation": "形容人全神贯注地盯着电视或手机屏幕看。", "example": "My little brother is always glued to the screen when his favorite cartoon is on.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Make headlines", "meaning": "登上头条 / 轰动一时", "explanation": "成为新闻的重要报道，引起广泛关注。", "example": "The release of the new smartphone made headlines yesterday.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What show on TV do you find most fascinating and why?", "Do you usually participate in any sports to unwind after school?", "Are you more of a couch potato or an active enthusiast on weekends?"]
transcoded_input: Honestly speaking, I spend too much time on my phone. These days, short videos are very addictive. I subscribe to many funny channels because they are entertaining. However, my parents think I should watch the news broadcast. They say it is more informative than cartoons. Sometimes, I agree with them. Actually, watching a good documentary is a great way to unwind. Although the commercials are annoying, the show itself is fascinating. In conclusion, I try not to be glued to the screen all day.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a memorable movie or TV show. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What it is and what happens in it.", "Who your favorite character is.", "Explain why you think it is worth watching."]
model_answer: Well, the most memorable show for me is 'Planet Earth'. Basically, it is a nature documentary that is broadcast on the BBC. To be honest, it is much more fascinating than a normal movie. I subscribe to their channel so I never miss an episode. The images are so beautiful that I am always glued to the screen. In fact, it often makes headlines around the world. Even though it is educational, it is also highly entertaining and addictive. All in all, it is the most informative show I have ever seen.

walt_content: Describe memorable meals and eating habits using specific vocabulary. Use the Present Perfect Tense to talk about life experiences with food.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "My favorite meal is ____ because..." and "I have never eaten ____." rely on Chinese translation table.
stretch_content: Use sensory adjectives (spicy, bitter, crunchy) to describe food in detail. Direct fast-finishers to Section 10.
grammar_content:
• Quantifiers: Use too much (uncountable) and too many (countable). (e.g., I ate too much cake / too many cookies).
量词：too much + 不可数名词; too many + 可数名词

• Present Perfect: Use have/has + V3 for experiences. (e.g., I have tried sushi before.).
现在完成时：have/has + 过去分词，表示经历
pronunciation_content:
Stretch out the vowel sounds in words like food (fuuuud), eat (eeet), and meal (meeeal). Do not cut them short!
长元音：拉长元音发音，不要读得太短促！
idioms: [{"phrase": "Have a sweet tooth", "meaning": "\u55dc\u751c\u5982\u547d / \u559c\u98df\u751c\u98df", "explanation": "\u6781\u5176\u559c\u6b22\u5403\u7cd6\u679c\u3001\u751c\u70b9\u7b49\u751c\u98df\u3002", "example": "I have a sweet tooth, so I always order chocolate cake after dinner.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "A piece of cake", "meaning": "\u5c0f\u83dc\u4e00\u789f / \u6613\u5982\u53cd\u638c", "explanation": "\u5f62\u5bb9\u505a\u67d0\u4ef6\u4e8b\u60c5\u975e\u5e38\u5bb9\u6613\uff0c\u6beb\u4e0d\u8d39\u529b\u3002", "example": "For a professional chef, cooking a big family dinner is a piece of cake.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What is the most entertaining cooking show you have ever watched? (Recycled vocab)", "Do you prefer homemade food or eating out at restaurants?", "Have you ever tried to cook a nutritious meal by yourself?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, I have always had a huge appetite. Recently, I watched an informative documentary about sugar, and I learned that it is very addictive. However, I have a sweet tooth, so it is hard for me to stop eating cakes. Usually, I try to consume healthy ingredients like vegetables and fish. Although fast food is tasty, I know that homemade food is much more nutritious. Actually, I have tried Japanese cuisine many times, and I love it. Overall, cooking healthy food is not a piece of cake, but it is worth it.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a memorable meal you have had. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Where you had it and who you were with.", "What specific dishes and ingredients you ate.", "Explain why this meal was so memorable for you."]
model_answer: Well, the most memorable meal I have ever had was a dinner at my grandmother's house. Normally, we visit her every Spring Festival. Actually, she is an amazing cook, and her homemade dumplings are delicious. To be honest, the ingredients she uses are always fresh from her garden. Because the food was so nutritious and tasty, I consumed three huge bowls! In fact, I have such a big appetite when I am there. To sum up, traditional Chinese cuisine is my favorite, and eating with family is never a piece of cake to forget. Also, learning about her recipes was very informative and the taste was truly addictive.

walt_content: Discuss journeys and transport modes. Use the First Conditional to talk about future travel possibilities.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "I usually travel by..." and "If I go to..., I will..."
stretch_content: Use adverbs of probability (definitely, probably) with the First Conditional.
grammar_content:
• First Conditional: If + Present Simple, ... will + Verb. (e.g., If it rains, I will take the bus.).
第一条件句：If + 一般现在时, ... will + 动词原形 (例如: If it rains, I will take the bus.)
pronunciation_content:
Stress the key words (verbs and nouns) in both parts of the sentence. Emphasize the result!
条件句中的重音：重读句子两部分中的关键词 (动词和名词)。强调结果！
idioms: [{"phrase": "Beat the traffic", "meaning": "\u9519\u5cf0\u51fa\u884c", "explanation": "\u63d0\u65e9\u51fa\u53d1\u4ee5\u907f\u5f00\u9053\u8def\u4ea4\u901a\u9ad8\u5cf0\u671f\u3002", "example": "We need to leave the house at 6 AM to beat the traffic.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Miss the boat", "meaning": "\u9519\u5931\u826f\u673a / \u5750\u5931\u673a\u5b9c", "explanation": "\u56e0\u4e3a\u884c\u52a8\u592a\u6162\u800c\u9519\u5931\u4e86\u826f\u673a\u3002", "example": "I forgot to buy the cheap train tickets yesterday, so I missed the boat.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) How do you usually commute to school every day?", "If you consume too much junk food before a long trip, how do you feel? (Recycled)", "Is the public transport in your city usually reliable?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, my daily commute to school is quite long. Usually, I take the subway because it is very convenient. However, during rush hour, there are too many passengers. If I wake up late tomorrow, I will definitely miss the bus and be late for class. Sometimes, my father drives me in his vehicle, but the congestion is terrible. Actually, we always try to leave early to beat the traffic. In my opinion, taking the subway is more reliable than driving. Also, it's safer because I don't consume fuel or pollute the air, which is not very nutritious for the planet.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a long journey you have taken. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Where you went and how you traveled.", "What happened during the journey.", "Explain how you felt about the trip."]
model_answer: Well, last summer I went to Beijing by train. Actually, it was a very long journey, taking over five hours. To be honest, the train was very comfortable and spacious for every passenger. However, we almost missed the boat because we arrived at the station late! If I visit Beijing again, I will definitely take the high-speed train to save time. Fortunately, the train was reliable and we arrived on time. Overall, despite the congestion near the station, it was a convenient way to travel. I also managed to consume a very nutritious meal on board.

walt_content: Describe unforgettable trips and travel experiences. Use Past Continuous vs Past Simple to tell stories.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly ONE yellow complex sentence.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "I went to ____." and "While I was walking, I saw..."
stretch_content: Use narrative tenses to create suspense or interest in your story.
grammar_content:
• Past Continuous: Was/Were + V-ing (Background action).
• Past Simple: V-ed (Interrupted action).
• While I was sleeping (continuous), the phone rang (simple).
过去进行时 vs 一般过去时：正在进行的背景动作被打断 (例如: While I was sleeping, the phone rang.)
pronunciation_content:
In fast speech, 'was' sounds like /wəz/ and 'were' sounds like /wə/. Don't stress them!
弱读：在快速语流中，was 读作 /wəz/，were 读作 /wə/。不要重读它们！
idioms: [{"phrase": "Travel light", "meaning": "\u8f7b\u88c5\u7b80\u884c / \u8f7b\u88c5\u4e0a\u9635", "explanation": "\u51fa\u95e8\u65c5\u884c\u65f6\u5c3d\u91cf\u5c11\u5e26\u884c\u674e\u3002", "example": "I always travel light because I hate carrying heavy bags at the airport.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Off the beaten track", "meaning": "\u4eba\u8ff9\u7f55\u81f3 / \u4e16\u5916\u6843\u6e90", "explanation": "\u53bb\u504f\u50fb\u7684\u3001\u5f88\u5c11\u6709\u6e38\u5ba2\u53bb\u7684\u51b7\u95e8\u5730\u65b9\u3002", "example": "We found a beautiful small beach that was completely off the beaten track.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What is your dream holiday destination?", "Is public transport convenient in your city for tourists? (Recycled)", "Do you prefer to plan a strict itinerary or just relax?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, I love traveling. Last year, I went to Yunnan, which is a popular destination. While I was looking for accommodation online, I found a small hotel off the beaten track. However, the traffic congestion on the way there was bad. While I was hiking up the mountain, I saw the most breathtaking view of the clouds. Actually, I prefer to travel light so I can explore easily. In the end, I bought a beautiful souvenir for my mom. It was convenient to carry home.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe an unforgettable trip you have taken. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Where you went and who you went with.", "What you did there.", "Explain why it was unforgettable."]
model_answer: Well, my most unforgettable trip was to Japan. Actually, I went there with my family two years ago. To be honest, we didn't plan a strict itinerary. While we were walking through Kyoto, we discovered a breathtaking old temple. Fortunately, our accommodation was very convenient and close to the subway. Also, I bought a traditional fan as a souvenir. To sum up, we loved to explore the city, even though we got lost once! We barely avoided the traffic congestion on the way to the airport.

walt_content: Describe school subjects and study habits. Use 'Used to' and 'Would' to talk about past educational experiences.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly TWO yellow complex sentences.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "My favorite subject is..." and "I used to study..."
stretch_content: Compare past and present education systems using sophisticated linking words.
grammar_content:
• Used to: For past states (e.g., I used to be a student) or habits.
• Would: ONLY for past habits/actions (e.g., I would study every night). NOT for states.
Used to vs Would：Used to 用于过去的状态或习惯；Would 只用于过去的习惯动作，不用于状态。
pronunciation_content:
• /t/ after voiceless sounds (e.g., watched)
• /d/ after voiced sounds (e.g., learned)
• /id/ after 't' or 'd' (e.g., decided)
-ed 结尾的发音：清辅音后发 /t/，浊辅音后发 /d/，t/d 后发 /id/。
idioms: [{"phrase": "Hit the books", "meaning": "\u57cb\u5934\u82e6\u8bfb / \u53d1\u594b\u56fe\u5f3a", "explanation": "\u5f00\u59cb\u975e\u5e38\u8ba4\u771f\u5730\u7528\u529f\u8bfb\u4e66\u590d\u4e60\u3002", "example": "I have a big math test tomorrow, so I need to hit the books tonight.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Learn by heart", "meaning": "\u719f\u8bb0\u4e8e\u5fc3 / \u5012\u80cc\u5982\u6d41", "explanation": "\u901a\u8fc7\u53cd\u590d\u8bb0\u5fc6\u5c06\u5185\u5bb9\u70c2\u719f\u4e8e\u5fc3\u3002", "example": "The teacher asked us to learn the short English poem by heart.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What was your favorite extracurricular activity in middle school?", "Did you have time to explore new hobbies last year? (Recycled)", "Do you find the current school curriculum difficult or easy?"]
transcoded_input: To be honest, I used to find school quite stressful. In the past, I had to complete a huge assignment every week. However, I was always very motivated to get a good qualification. Although the curriculum was hard, I would always concentrate in class to understand the lessons. Also, I loved extracurricular activities like painting. Actually, art class allowed me to explore my creativity. Sometimes, I created breathtaking drawings. Because I wanted to improve, I would often hit the books until late at night. In short, hard work pays off.
circuit_prompt_intro: Describe a favorite subject you studied at school. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What the subject was.", "Why you enjoyed it.", "How it has helped you."]
model_answer: Well, my favorite subject used to be Geography. Actually, I loved learning about the world and different cultures. To be honest, I would always concentrate fully when the teacher showed us maps. Since the world is so big, I wanted to explore every breathtaking landscape. Moreover, I was motivated to memorize all the capital cities by heart. In addition, the curriculum included interesting assignments about climate change. Even though the exams were difficult, getting a high qualification in Geography made me very proud. To sum up, it was a fascinating subject.

walt_content: Discuss future careers and job requirements. Use Modal Verbs of Obligation to describe duties and rules.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly TWO yellow complex sentences.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "I want to be a..." and "A doctor must..."
stretch_content: Use formal vocabulary to describe job responsibilities and career paths.
grammar_content:
• Must: Strong internal obligation (I must study).
• Have to: External obligation/rule (I have to wear a uniform).

support_content: Sentence frames: "Having saved money, I..." and "Working hard, he..."
stretch_content: Discuss the ethics of wealth distribution and inequality.
grammar_content:
• Present: Walking home, I saw a cat. (Actions at same time).
• Past (Perfect): Having finished work, I left. (One action before another).
分词从句：用 V-ing 或 Having + V3 代替连词，使句子更简洁高级。
pronunciation_content:
Pause after the clause to separate it from the main sentence.
• Having finished (pause), I went out.
分词从句的停顿：在分词短语后稍作停顿，与主句分开。
idioms: [{"phrase": "Bread and butter", "meaning": "\u996d\u7897 / \u8c0b\u751f\u4e4b\u9053", "explanation": "\u8c0b\u751f\u4e4b\u9053\uff0c\u6700\u4e3b\u8981\u7684\u6536\u5165\u6765\u6e90\u6216\u57fa\u672c\u4e8b\u7269\u3002", "example": "Teaching English online is my main bread and butter at the moment.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Save for a rainy day", "meaning": "\u672a\u96e8\u7ef8\u7f2a / \u6709\u5907\u65e0\u60a3", "explanation": "\u5b58\u94b1\u6216\u4fdd\u7559\u8d44\u6e90\u4ee5\u5907\u4e0d\u65f6\u4e4b\u9700\u3002", "example": "Instead of buying a new phone, I decided to save my money for a rainy day.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) What are the consequences of a materialistic society?", "Is your current job lucrative enough for you? (Recycled)", "How do you allocate your monthly budget?"]
transcoded_input: It is obvious that money matters. However, there is a huge disparity between rich and poor. Actually, we live in a very materialistic world. Having observed society, I see that people buy things to compensate for sadness. Ideally, wealth should be allocated more fairly. Some jobs are indispensable but pay poorly. Working hard, everyone should be able to flourish. Unfortunately, even lucrative jobs are stressful. Managers often fail to delegate work. We should remember that money is just bread and butter, not everything. Always save for a rainy day.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss the problem of wealth inequality. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Why there is a gap.", "How it affects people.", "What can be done."]
model_answer: It is undeniable that economic disparity is growing. Actually, the rich get richer while the poor struggle. To be honest, being materialistic doesn't make us happy. Having realized this, many people are seeking a simpler life. However, money is indispensable for survival. Being a lucrative business, tech companies should compensate their workers better. If we want society to flourish, we must allocate resources wisely. Leaders need to delegate power to the community. To sum up, fairness is key.

transcoded_input: It is shocking that athletes earn so much. Rarely do we see such high salaries in other jobs. Actually, brands pay them millions to endorse products. Never have I seen an amateur player gain such prestige, even if they work harder. However, athletes must persevere through pain. They exert enormous energy. Little do people realize that team solidarity is indispensable for success. If sports are to flourish, we need fairness. Don't throw in the towel just because it's hard. Always give it your best shot.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss the salaries of professional athletes. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Why they earn so much.", "If it is fair.", "The impact on society."]
model_answer: It is often debated whether athletes are overpaid. Actually, their careers are short. To be honest, rarely do people work as physically hard as they do. Not only do they exert themselves daily, but they also inspire solidarity among fans. However, teachers are also indispensable but earn less. Seldom have I seen a teacher flourish financially like a sports star. Brands endorse athletes for their prestige. Even an amateur must persevere. To sum up, the market decides.

transcoded_input: It seems that technology connects us. However, screens can alienate people. Actually, online chats are often superficial. What we need is more empathy towards others, especially those who are vulnerable. Some people hide behind avatars. To build a harmonious society, we must nurture real connections. It is trust that allows relationships to persevere through hard times. I don't endorse cyberbullying. True friends stick together through thick and thin. We should always see the best in someone.

I endorse putting phones away. To sum up, real feelings need real presence.

grammar_content:
• Wishes: I wish I were (not 'was') rich.
• Demands: It is essential that he be (not 'is') on time.

虚拟语气：用于表达愿望、建议或非真实情况。注意 be 动词常用 were 或原形 be。
pronunciation_content:
Use a sighing or longing tone when expressing wishes.
• I wiiish I were rich... (Elongate 'wish')

idioms: [{"phrase": "Do one's bit", "meaning": "\u5c3d\u7ef5\u8584\u4e4b\u529b / \u5c3d\u672c\u5206", "explanation": "\u5c3d\u81ea\u5df1\u7684\u4e00\u4efd\u529b\u91cf\u6216\u8d23\u4efb\u3002", "example": "Everyone should do their bit to help clean up the local park.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Step up to the plate", "meaning": "\u633a\u8eab\u800c\u51fa / \u52c7\u6311\u91cd\u62c5", "explanation": "\u633a\u8eab\u800c\u51fa\uff0c\u627f\u62c5\u8d23\u4efb\u6216\u5e94\u5bf9\u6311\u6218\u3002", "example": "When the manager quit, she stepped up to the plate and led the team.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]

transcoded_input: It is essential that society helps the poor. However, many groups are still marginalized. Actually, philanthropy is not just about giving money. It is vital that big companies be more altruistic and support local initiatives. I wish everyone were willing to do their bit. Personally, I try to show empathy to those who are vulnerable. If we were to mobilize our resources, we could empower many people. It is time for leaders to step up to the plate. We suggest that every student join a volunteer club.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss the role of charity in society. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["Who should be responsible for helping.", "The benefits of volunteering.", "Challenges charities face."]

To be honest, I wish the government were more active in helping marginalized groups. It is crucial that we empower vulnerable people rather than just giving them handouts. Also, showing empathy is key. If everyone were to mobilize for a common initiative, society would change. We need altruistic leaders to step up to the plate. To sum up, we must all do our bit.

idioms: [{"phrase": "Against all odds", "meaning": "\u6392\u9664\u4e07\u96be / \u9006\u5883\u4e2d\u53d6\u80dc", "explanation": "\u5728\u9762\u4e34\u6781\u5927\u56f0\u96be\u6216\u770b\u4f3c\u6beb\u65e0\u5e0c\u671b\u7684\u60c5\u51b5\u4e0b\u53d6\u5f97\u4e86\u6210\u529f\u3002", "example": "Against all odds, he managed to build a successful business from scratch.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Go the extra mile", "meaning": "\u52a0\u500d\u52aa\u529b / \u7cbe\u76ca\u6c42\u7cbe", "explanation": "\u4ed8\u51fa\u6bd4\u9884\u671f\u6216\u8981\u6c42\u66f4\u591a\u7684\u52aa\u529b\u3002", "example": "She is an amazing student who always goes the extra mile with her homework.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]

transcoded_input: It is widely recognized that success takes time. Actually, having tenacious habits is key. Many people strive to reach the pinnacle of their career. Having faced many failures, they develop the resilience needed to continue. However, talent is not enough. You must be willing to go the extra mile. I appreciate being empowered by my teachers to try new things. Some athletes have achieved unprecedented records. Actually, they surpass expectations against all odds. In my opinion, mobilizeing your inner strength is vital.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss the importance of resilience. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What resilience means.", "A time you were resilient.", "Why it leads to success."]
model_answer: It is often said that resilience is more important than IQ. Actually, life is full of challenges. To be honest, having failed a math exam once, I felt terrible. However, being supported by my parents helped me mobilize my energy to study again. I decided to strive for better grades. Having worked tenaciously for months, I eventually surpassed my classmates. Reaching the pinnacle of the class was an unprecedented feeling for me. I learned that you can succeed against all odds if you go the extra mile. To sum up, never give up.

idioms: [{"phrase": "A drop in the ocean", "meaning": "\u6ca7\u6d77\u4e00\u7c9f / \u676f\u6c34\u8f66\u85aa", "explanation": "\u4e0e\u6240\u9700\u91cf\u76f8\u6bd4\u5fae\u4e0d\u8db3\u9053\u7684\u4e00\u70b9\u70b9\u3002", "example": "My small donation is just a drop in the ocean, but I hope it helps.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Wipe out", "meaning": "\u5f7b\u5e95\u6d88\u706d / \u6bb7\u7edd", "explanation": "\u5f7b\u5e95\u6467\u6bc1\u6216\u6d88\u706d\u67d0\u7269\u3002", "example": "If we don't stop polluting, we might wipe out entire species of animals.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]

transcoded_input: It is undeniable that our planet is suffering. Actually, we have caused unprecedented damage. The more resources we use, the faster we deplete the earth. The harder we try to grow the economy, the more detrimental the ecological impact becomes. Sadly, some damage is irreversible. We risk wiping out vital biodiversity. The longer we wait, the harder it will be to mitigate the disaster. Although nature has resilience, it has limits. My efforts feel like a drop in the ocean. However, we must act now.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss the urgency of climate action. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["What the main threats are.", "How our actions are linked to results.", "What must be done."]
model_answer: It is widely agreed that time is running out. Actually, the loss of biodiversity is alarming. To be honest, unprecedented storms are becoming common. The more we deplete forests, the less resilience the planet has against carbon. We must mitigate these detrimental effects before they become irreversible. The sooner we act, the better chance we have to avoid wiping out species. Individual acts may seem like a drop in the ocean, but together they matter. To sum up, our ecological future is in our hands.

support_content: Sentence frames: "I was going to..., but..."
stretch_content: Discuss the 'Paradigm Shift' caused by AI.
grammar_content:
• Structure: Was/Were going to + Verb.
• Use: Plans in the past that usually didn't happen.
• I was going to buy it, but it was too expensive.
过去将来时：Was/Were going to...。表示过去原本打算做某事（通常后来没做成）。
pronunciation_content:
Use a falling intonation on the first part (plan), and a rising intonation on 'but' (contrast).
• I was going to GO (↘), BUT (↗)...
转折的语调：前半句（计划）用降调，but（转折）用升调。
idioms: [{"phrase": "Light years ahead", "meaning": "\u9065\u9065\u9886\u5148 / \u671b\u5c18\u83ab\u53ca", "explanation": "\u5728\u53d1\u5c55\u6216\u6280\u672f\u4e0a\u9065\u9065\u9886\u5148\u4e8e\u5176\u4ed6\u4eba\u3002", "example": "The new AI technology is light years ahead of what we had ten years ago.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Reinvent the wheel", "meaning": "\u591a\u6b64\u4e00\u4e3e / \u91cd\u65b0\u53d1\u660e\u8f6e\u5b50", "explanation": "\u767d\u8d39\u529b\u6c14\u53bb\u91cd\u65b0\u53d1\u660e\u5df2\u7ecf\u5b58\u5728\u4e14\u6709\u6548\u7684\u4e1c\u897f\u3002", "example": "The software already works perfectly, so there is no need to reinvent the wheel.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]

They were going to automate the library system, but the algorithm was too sophisticated. Now, AI is a new paradigm. Some people worry it will cause irreversible job losses. We were going to mitigate this by training staff, but the change is happening too fast. Actually, this tech is light years ahead of old tools. We shouldn't reinvent the wheel; we should use it. Technology is now indispensable.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss how technology changes plans. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["A plan you had involving tech.", "Why it changed.", "The outcome."]

We were going to automate customer service, but we needed a more sophisticated algorithm. This shift represents a new paradigm. Although the delay was frustrating, we managed to mitigate the loss and build something indispensable. Our new AI is light years ahead. To sum up, adaptation is key to avoid irreversible failure.

walt_content: Critique cultural trends. Use Ellipsis & Substitution to avoid repetition.
wilf_content:
1. Exactly TWO yellow complex sentences.

2. At least TWO blue sentence starters.

3. Accurate use of the bold weekly vocabulary.
support_content: Sentence frames: "I think so." and "I don't."
stretch_content: Analyze the loss of indigenous languages.
grammar_content:
• Ellipsis: Omit words. Do you like it? I do (like it).
• Substitution: Replace with 'one/ones/so'. I like the red one. I hope so.
省略与替代：Ellipsis (省略重复词), Substitution (用 one/so 替代)。
pronunciation_content:
Stress the auxiliary verb in short answers.
• Yes, I DO.
• No, I DON'T.
简短回答的重音：重读助动词。
idioms: [{"phrase": "A melting pot", "meaning": "\u5927\u7194\u7089", "explanation": "\u5927\u7194\u7089\uff0c\u6307\u4e0d\u540c\u6587\u5316\u3001\u79cd\u65cf\u878d\u5408\u7684\u5730\u65b9\u3002", "example": "New York City is known around the world as a cultural melting pot.", "bg_color": "#fef9e7"}, {"phrase": "Culture shock", "meaning": "\u6587\u5316\u51b2\u51fb", "explanation": "\u5728\u8fdb\u5165\u964c\u751f\u7684\u6587\u5316\u73af\u5883\u4e2d\u611f\u5230\u7684\u56f0\u60d1\u548c\u4e0d\u5b89\u3002", "example": "When I first moved to China from the UK, I experienced a lot of culture shock.", "bg_color": "#e8f8f5"}]
warmup_questions: ["(Section 5 Question) Will globalization inevitably homogenize diverse cultures?", "Is the Western cultural paradigm too dominant? (Recycled)", "Do you want to assimilate into a new culture if you move abroad?"]
transcoded_input: It is feared that globalization destroys diversity. Some people think we will all become the same. I hope not. Actually, big brands tend to homogenize cities. They sell sophisticated products that everyone wants, but this creates a boring paradigm. We must preserve our unique traditions. If we don't, we lose our linguistic roots. Immigrants often try to assimilate, but they shouldn't lose their identity. Don't believe every stereotype you hear. Some are true, but many aren't. Our city is a melting pot. Avoid culture shock by being open.
circuit_prompt_intro: Discuss the future of culture. You should say:
circuit_prompt_points: ["If cultures are becoming the same.", "Why this happens.", "How to keep traditions alive."]
model_answer: It is often asked if we are losing our identity. I think so. Actually, media tends to homogenize fashion and food. To be honest, this paradigm endangers linguistic diversity. People want to be sophisticated, so they copy global trends. However, patriotism can help us preserve customs. We welcome those who want to assimilate, but we also value the old ways. Living in a melting pot helps reduce stereotypes. Will we lose everything? I hope not. To sum up, diversity matters.
